author: Chung Kun Tao
description: Import your Kobo highlights into Obsidian: one clean note per book, sorted by true reading order and grouped by chapter, with your own notes protected. Reads from a connected Kobo eReader or the Kobo desktop app. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Doug (fire-university)
manifest.description: Import your Kobo highlights as clean Markdown notes: one note per book, sorted by true reading order and grouped by chapter, with your own notes protected. Reads from a connected Kobo eReader or the Kobo desktop app.
manifest.name: PaperFolio for Kobo

name: PaperFolio for Kobo

releases.author: fire-university
releases.description:
Fixes reported by the community directory's automated review.

- Removed the word "Obsidian" from the plugin description
- Replaced the `builtin-modules` dependency with Node's own `module.builtinModules`
- Removed `localStorage` usage: language auto-detection now uses the officially exported `moment.locale()`

No functional changes.

releases.author: fire-university
releases.description:
First public release.

Import your Kobo highlights into Obsidian: one clean note per book, sorted by true reading order and grouped by real chapter names, with your own writing protected.

**Sources**
- Kobo eReader over USB
- Kobo desktop app (no cable)
- Optional wireless sync over your LAN, so the Kobo can push highlights with one tap

**Highlights**
- Sorted by position in the book, not by when you highlighted
- Real chapter names, recovered from the book's EPUB when the database has none
- Your own notes outside the sentinel block are never touched
- Incremental and de-duplicated, with an auto-generated index note
- English and Traditional Chinese

Desktop-only. No accounts, no servers, no telemetry.
